summary: The Reed-Wood conjecture was proved by Csoka, Lo, Norin, Wu, and Yepremyan (2015 arXiv; published JCTB 2017). Their paper 'The extremal function for disconnected minors' establishes that every graph with average degree at least $\frac{4}{3}t-2$ contains every 2-regular graph on $t$ vertices as a minor, verifying the Reed-Wood (and Harvey-Wood) conjectures for unions of cycles. The bound is tight by the $K_{2k-1,n}$ examples noted in the OPG discussion.

since_posted.title: The extremal function for disconnected minors
since_posted.authors: Endre Csoka, Irene Lo, Sergey Norin, Hehui Wu, Liana Yepremyan
since_posted.claim: Proves that every graph with average degree at least 4t/3 - 2 contains every 2-regular graph on t vertices as a minor, settling the Reed-Wood conjecture (and the related Harvey-Wood conjecture for unions of cycles).

since_posted.title: Average degree conditions forcing a minor
since_posted.authors: Daniel J. Harvey, David R. Wood
since_posted.claim: Strengthens Reed-Wood bounds for sparse graphs with many high-degree vertices and provides earlier partial progress toward the 2-regular minor conjecture, motivating the Csoka et al. resolution.

search_queries: Reed Wood "forcing a sparse minor" 2-regular minor average degree
"2-regular minor" average degree conjecture Reed Wood
extremal function disconnected minor Csoka Lo Norin Wu Yepremyan
notes: The OPG record itself flags [CNLWY] as recent progress; verification of the arXiv PDF confirms Theorem 1 of Csoka-Lo-Norin-Wu-Yepremyan explicitly states and proves the Reed-Wood conjecture (every graph with average degree at least 4t/3 - 2 contains every 2-regular graph on t vertices as a minor). The journal version appeared in J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 126 (2017), 162-174, doi:10.1016/j.jctb.2017.04.005. Sciencedirect/Elsevier abstract page returned 403 to WebFetch but the DOI redirect resolved to the same article and the arXiv preprint was directly verified.
